titlestmt.titleproper: Mary Merritt Crawford and Family papers41-5-619
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-08-26 18:32:55 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Mary Merritt Crawford and Family papers
did.abstract: Includes campaign material and reports to the Cornell Alumni Corporation (1932-37) upon completion of each of her terms as alumni trustee; tape recordings and a partial transcript of interviews with Dr. Crawford (1962, 1964) concerning her life as a Cornell co-ed, her career as a doctor, and her service on the Board of Trustees. Also, correspondence, notes, photographs, and printed material concerning alumni affairs, including lists of Cornell women graduates (1873-88) and biographical and bibliographical data compiled by Bessie Bell DeWitt (Mrs. Willard Beahan) and printed material compiled by Simon Henry Gage, Professor of Histology and Embryology, on Susanna Phelps Gage ('80), Mary Hannah Gage-Day, M.D., Theobald Smith ('81), Professor of Histology and Embryology, and the first faculty of the Veterinary College. Medical hood worn by Dr. Crawford as a Cornell Trustee.

Papers of Mary Merritt Crawford include student scrapbook, 1900-1904; diaries; photographs, including spring day; and extensive correspondence, 1887-1973, between Mary and other family members especially her parents; her sisters, Charlotte, Lucy, and Caroline; her husband Edward Schuster; and her daughter Mary Schuster Jaffe. Of particular interest are the letters she wrote to her family while she was serving in the Ambulance Corps in France during World War I. Many have typescript copies.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Some letters by Mary Merritt Crawford written while she was a surgeon at the American Ambulance Hospital in France were published in Vol. I, No. 2-5 (1915-16) of the Cornell Women's Review.

emph.ref: Series II. Caroline Cecilia Crawford Wolff
Series VI. Mary Crawford Schuster Jaffe
Series VII. Crawford Family Member Papers

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Mary Merritt Crawford Papers, #41-5-619. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Physician, Cornell University alumni trustee. Mary Merritt Crawford graduated from Cornell in 1904, and received her M.D. in 1907. She was later elected alumni trustee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Includes Crawford family photographs and correspondence. Mary Merritt Crawford materials include: campaign material and reports to the Cornell Alumni Corporation (1932-37) upon completion of each of her terms as alumni trustee; tape recordings and a partial transcript of interviews with Dr. Crawford (1962, 1964) concerning her life as a Cornell co-ed, her career as a doctor, and her service on the Board of Trustees. Also, correspondence, notes, photographs, and printed material concerning alumni affairs, including lists of Cornell women graduates (1873-88) and biographical and bibliographical data compiled by Bessie Bell DeWitt (Mrs. Willard Beahan) and printed material compiled by Simon Henry Gage, Professor of Histology and Embryology, on Susanna Phelps Gage ('80), Mary Hannah Gage-Day, M.D., Theobald Smith ('81), Professor of Histology and Embryology, and the first faculty of the Veterinary College. Medical hood worn by Dr. Crawford as a Cornell Trustee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers of Mary Merritt Crawford include student scrapbook, 1900-1904; diaries; photographs, including spring day; and extensive correspondence, 1887-1973, between Mary and other family members especially her parents; her sisters, Charlotte, Lucy, and Caroline; her husband Edward Schuster; and her daughter Mary Schuster Jaffe. Of particular interest are the letters she wrote to her family while she was serving in the Ambulance Corps in France during World War I. Many have typescript copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: May contain small amounts of outgoing correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Sarah Crawford with clipping of Mary's hair

Correspondence between Mary Merritt Crawford and Edward Schuster

American Women's Association correspondence, speeches, and documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photo book from the retirement party.

Articles and clippings on or by Mary Merritt Crawford

scopecontent.p: Notebook recording events and times at Williamsburgh Hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary with photographs

did.unittitle: "Quick Steps in Botany" by Luis Otis
Scrabook containing newspaper clippings about Mary Merritt Crawford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains article on Mary Merritt Crawford.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Anna Botsford Comstock about the trustee election (1925), letters from Hendrik van Loon, Ezra Day, Romeyn Berry, Simon Gage, copies of letters from Henry Sage, Martha Van Rensselaer, Henry de Forest, Frank Gannett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains ephemera, clippings, and correspondence from Crawford's student years at Cornell, including: sports programs, sorority and fraternity invitations, sketches, glee club programs, photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Crawford in medical uniform, during surgeries, and with patients.

did.unittitle: Photographs of patients and hospital in France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one photograph in operating theater, X-ray of skull with embedded bullet, and Board of Trustees (1931)

scopecontent.p: Undergraduate scrapbook containing invitations, dance banquet and theater programs, Adelphia material, correspondence and personal notes, photographs and printed material

scopecontent.p: Two copies.

scopecontent.p: Also includes some correspondence to Gilbert Holmes Crawford.

Letters from Mary Merritt Crawford, typed copies

scopecontent.p: Mainly contains correspondence on Conrad Crawford's death.
